articles.title: Meet the Wall-Crawling Spider Named After Pink Floyd That Punches Above Its Weight
articles.summary: A newly discovered spider, Pikelinia floydmuraria, lives in building walls and hunts prey up to six times its size. Named after Pink Floyd, it targets household pests like mosquitoes and flies, potentially helping urban pest control.

articles.title: Forget Heart Rate: Scientists Are Now Tracking Your Farts with Smart Underwear
articles.summary: Researchers developed a quarter-sized sensor that snaps into underwear to measure hydrogen from farts. A pilot study found people average 32 toots daily, but the range is wide. The Human Flatus Atlas project aims to establish a normal baseline.

articles.title: The $1 Cyberattack Is Here: How AI Is Reshaping the Hacker-Defender Arms Race
articles.summary: Large language models can now autonomously discover and exploit software vulnerabilities for under a dollar. While AI also aids defenders, the asymmetry favors attackers because fixing bugs still requires human effort, especially in under-resourced open-source projects.
